methods.EvalTo(): Evaluates this quantity and returns a new slice containing the result.
methods.EvalTo(Slice):
Evaluates this quantity into the provided slice object.
A slice can, for instance, be created with `NewScalarMask`, `NewVectorMask` or `LoadFile`.
methods.Add(float64, Config): Adds a scaled configuration to the current configuration.
methods.Add(Shape): Add this shape to the original shape.
methods.Add(Slice, ScalarFunction): Add an extra mask*multiplier term to the excitation.
methods.RotZ(float64): Rotate the shape about the Z-axis by a given angle in radians.
methods.Scale(float64, float64, float64): Scales the magnetization or configuration by the provided factors.
methods.Transl(float64, float64, float64): Translates a vector configuration in x, y, and z directions.
methods.Intersect(Shape): Intersection between this shape and the original shape.
methods.Repeat(float64, float64, float64): Repeat the shape every (dx, dy, dz) in space.
methods.RotX(float64): Rotate the shape about the X-axis by a given angle in radians.
methods.RotY(float64): Rotate the shape about the Y-axis by a given angle in radians.
methods.Sub(Shape): Subtract this shape from the original shape.
methods.Xor(Shape): Exclusive OR with the original shape.
methods.Average(): Returns the spatial average of this quantity across the entire mesh.
methods.Div(float64): Divide the vector by a given amount.
methods.Len():
If called on a
- Slice: returns the number of elements per component
- Vector: returns its norm.
methods.MAdd(float64, data.Vector): Multiply by float64 and add a Vector to the result.
methods.Mul(float64): Multiply this vector by a scalar value.
methods.X(): Get the X-component of this vector.
methods.Y(): Get the Y-component of this vector.
methods.Z(): Get the Z-component of this vector.
methods.CPUAccess():
Returns whether the Slice is accessible by the CPU.
 `true` means it is stored in host memory.
methods.Comp(int): Accesses a component of a vector quantity.
methods.DevPtr(int): Returns a CUDA device pointer to a component. Slice must have GPUAccess. It is safe to call on a nil slice, returns NULL.
methods.Disable(): INTERNAL. Overwrite struct fields with zeros to avoid accidental use after Free.
methods.Free(): Frees the GPU memory and disables the slice.
methods.GPUAccess():
Returns whether the Slice is accessible by the GPU.
 `true` means it is either stored on GPU or in unified host memory.
methods.Get(int, int, int, int): Get the value at (comp, ix, iy, iz) of this slice.
methods.Get(): Returns the average value, either as `float64` or `data.Vector`.
methods.Host(): Returns the slice as type `[][]float32` indexed by component and cell number. Requires `CPUAccess() == true`.
methods.HostCopy(): Returns a copy of the Slice, allocated on CPU.
methods.Index(int, int, int): Returns the flat index of (ix, iy, iz).
methods.IsNil(): Returns `true` if either this slice or its pointer are `nil`.
methods.MemType(): Returns 1 (2) if this slice resides on the CPU (GPU).
methods.Scalars(): Returns the data as 3D array (type `[][][]float32`), indexed by cell position. Data should be scalar (1 component) and have `CPUAccess() == true`.
methods.Set(int, int, int, int, float64): Set the value at (comp, ix, iy, iz) of this slice to a given scalar value.
methods.Set(float64): Sets this quantity to a uniform scalar value in all regions.
methods.Set(data.Vector): Set this excitation to a constant vector.
methods.Set(Config): Set the magnetization to the given configuration.
methods.SetScalar(int, int, int, float64): Set the value at index (ix, iy, iz) of a scalar slice.
methods.SetVector(int, int, int, data.Vector): Set the value at index (ix, iy, iz) of a vector slice.
methods.Size(): Returns the size of this slice as a `[3]int`.
methods.Tensors(): Returns the data as 4D array (type `[][][][]float32`), indexed by component and cell position. Requires `CPUAccess() == true`.
methods.Vectors(): Returns the data as 4D array (type `[3][][][]float32`), indexed by component and cell position. Data should have 3 components and have `CPUAccess() == true`.
methods.GetRegion(int): Returns the value of this quantity within the specified region.
methods.IsUniform(): Returns true if the field is uniform across all cells.
methods.MSlice(): Get the underlying `cuda.MSlice` of this quantity.
methods.Region(int): Access the value of this quantity in the given region.
methods.SetRegion(int, ScalarFunction): Set the value of this quantity in the given region to a scalar function.
methods.SetRegion(int, VectorFunction): Set the value of this quantity in the given region to a vector function.
methods.SetRegion(int, Config): Set the magnetization to the given configuration inside the specified region.
methods.SetRegionFuncGo(int, func() float64): Set the value of this quantity in the given region to a scalar function. Should only be used in a Go script.
methods.SetRegionValueGo(int, float64): Set the value of this quantity in the given region to a fixed scalar value. Should only be used in a Go script.
methods.SetRegionFn(int, func() [3]float64): Set the value of this excitation in a given region. Should only be used in a Go script.
methods.AddGo(Slice, func() float64): An `Add(mask, f)` equivalent for Go use.
methods.AddTo(Slice): Add the values of this field to the provided slice.
methods.RemoveExtraTerms(): After resizing the mesh, the extra terms don't fit the grid anymore and there is no reasonable way to resize them. So remove them and have the user re-add them.
methods.GetCell(int, int, int): Get the value at index (ix, iy, iz).
methods.Gpu(): Return a slice that lives on the GPU.
methods.Buffer(): Return a slice that lives on the GPU.
methods.LoadFile(string): Load from a previously saved file.
methods.Quantity(): Return the average magnetization as type `[]float64`.
methods.SetArray(Slice): Set the magnetization based on the provided slice.
methods.SetCell(int, int, int, data.Vector): Set the magnetization at index (ix, iy, iz) to a given vector.
methods.SetCell(int, int, int, int): Set the region at index (ix, iy, iz) to a particular value (last argument).
methods.SetInShape(Shape, Config): Set the magnetization to a given configuration, but only inside the provided shape.
methods.HostArray(): Returns the regions throughout the mesh as type `[][][]byte`.
methods.HostList(): Returns the regions throughout the flattened mesh as type `[]byte`.
